q.name: lNot happy with the breed results because it not even close. Tried to sell me the genetic breed test…

content.p: lNot happy with the breed results because it not even close. Tried to sell me the genetic breed test again after already buying it. Wouldn't give me any results for the genetic age unless I told them how old I thought the dog is (I don't know). Thet made me give them a guess then gave me a genetic age which I believe is way wrong too. The reason is that we've owned our dog for 4 years and he was an adult when we rescued him years ago. We believe him to be somewhere between 6 and 10. Well, this company gave me a genetic age of 3! Do the math and it doesn't add up ... not even closely.
I smell a rip-off. Waste of money and time.

q.name: What is your dog?

content.p: What is your dog?

q.name: I wanted to find out what my Rescue Dog’s Breed and Genetic age was and saw this kit and I liked the…

content.p: I wanted to find out what my Rescue Dog’s Breed and Genetic age was and saw this kit and I liked the option of the age test. The customer support is terrific and they have been helpful in assistance with the test results and checking on test status. But I was disappointed in the fact there is not a breakdown with percentages of each breed only levels. The span is 75% to 100% level 1, 37% to 74% level 2, 20% to 36% level 3. Since it is not listed with percentages I thought maybe alphabetical order. When I wrote for a more detailed breakdown I was given it after I gave my dogs weight. He is a 65 pound boy listed as a Boston Terrier and American Staffordshire Terrier. So after I gave his weight, although I was advised it was to aid in the genetic age test, I was then told he was 61% American Staffordshire Terrier and 39% Boston Terrier. Weird. But I always thought he was an AmStaff I found it odd the listing came back opposite and not in alphabetical order and that was explained as they don’t update the alphabetical order when they add new breeds and only recently (4 years ago) added testing for American Staffordshire Terriers. So if you are looking for a more detailed breakdown unless you keep in contact with customer support this is not the kit for you.

q.name: Results Are Highly Questionable

content.p: Results Are Highly Questionable

q.name: Early Reviewer Rewards

content.p: Early Reviewer Rewards

q.name: I bought two of these tests, one for each of my dogs. The results that I got back are highly suspect…

content.p: I bought two of these tests, one for each of my dogs. The results that I got back are highly suspect. They claim that the DNA of my new rescue dog is part Boxer and part Pembroke Welsh Corgi when he highly resembles a Basenji mix, right down to the curled tail. They claim lesser parts of Beagle and Poodle. Of course, they couldn't see the tell- tale Basenji curled tail in the picture that I uploaded, and I didn't tell them about that major trait. His coloring is similar to a Pembroke Welsh Corgi, which makes me wonder if they looked at his picture and made a best guess on his breed rather than performing the DNA test. This company also has the same product under two other company names, Dog DNA and DNA Affirm, and there is a review worth reading under DNA Affirm about this company, and other reviews there question the authenticity of the results as well. Since this company is in Canada, they are possibly not subject to the restrictions for false advertising that companies in the U.S. are. I just purchased another DNA dog test from a company here in the United States and will update this review upon learning those results. If I could get my money back for the two kits that I purchased, I would. I think this company is not a legitimate business.

I just got back the results for the second dog I tested. I purchased this test to try to determine his age. He spent two years at the Rescue before I adopted him, and he was about 2 years old when he arrived there, and we have had him 8 years, so we figure that he is at least 12 years old. He is quite elderly with COPD, arthritis, cataracts, and a virus that produces warts that bleed. His picture I uploaded shows him fairly vibrant about five years ago. The report says his genetic age is 8 years old, which doesn't seem right at all given his current physical appearance. The DNA breed tests claim that he is part bloodhound, part pointer, part Golden Retriever, and part Rhodesian Ridgeback when in actuality he is actually a Black and Tan Coonhound purebred. I didn’t buy the test for his breed; I bought it to try to determine his actual age, which could be anywhere from 12- 15 years old. I consider the money spent here a complete waste of money and would encourage others to use one of the other companies that do this test in the United States. They are more expensive, but I think they are legitimate businesses. I just purchase one from Embark for the newest Rescue and will update this review to see if their information contradicts this one.

q.name: Early Reviewer Rewards

content.p: Early Reviewer Rewards

q.name: Worked great. You should know that if you have a mixed breed like me, your results will come back in…

content.p: Worked great. You should know that if you have a mixed breed like me, your results will come back in percentage ranges, such as 20%-36% Great Pyrenees. This is not an exact science. Anyway this was a Lot of fun! Not only did I receive 5 breeds and the percentage of each my dog might be, the company provided a good bit of info about each breed and their temperaments. Explained a lot lol. And they provided potential health issues my dog might be susceptible to, such as hip dysplaysia, and for each breed. I learned that my "bloodhound mix" was really 2/3 two different breeds of coon hound, and had some St. Bernard in him too. No wonder he's huge! Well worth the price, in my opinion. I will add that It was a little challenging to hold a large dog down to swap both the left and then later the right side of the inside of his cheeks for approx 30 or 45 seconds, I forget how long exactly, but it was such a challenging experience that it seemed like an eternity lol. The results were provided by email and website info within the 2 week period that was promised. I was disappointed that I did not receive his age, as promised. However, I would do this again. If my review helped you at all, pls do give me a helpful vote, thanks so much!

q.name: This is the 3rd Orivet DNA test kit we've tried in 6 weeks. So far they have lost two of the kits an…

content.p: This is the 3rd Orivet DNA test kit we've tried in 6 weeks. So far they have lost two of the kits and miraculously found one after 5 weeks. Yet 10 days after finding the first kit,
there are still no results available - mind you that kit was mailed on Feb 2 and today is March 9th.

The second kit is still out in the ether. Calling and emailing multiple times rendered 1 response stating I must not have sent these in. We're going back to the Embark brand tests where they actually do the testing.

q.name: Results received - very cool!!

content.p: Results received - very cool!!

q.name: 2/14/20: Kit was received 2/19/20: Mailed out samples 2/27/20: Received email from Orivet that sampl…

content.p: 2/14/20: Kit was received
2/19/20: Mailed out samples
2/27/20: Received email from Orivet that samples were received

3/22/20: Wondering what the status was, checked on the website, to find that my results had been available since 3/16/20. However, it appears that only one of my bar codes is finished, the other is still pending?
2/14: My experience so far - this kit was a little confusing to use. Step one, activate the kit. I went on their website, entered my info, and submitted. That was easy enough.

Then I read the instructions to swab my dog and apply the bar code labels. However, the instructions only mentioned if you have one set of bar codes - I had two different numbers. I went back to the website and read through their instructions, FAQs, everything - no mention of what to do with the second bar code. I finally ended up emailing customer service, who got back to me about 2 days after I emailed them. They indicated to put one barcode on each packet of swabs, and write both barcodes on the envelope.

Once I figured out the bar code deal, it was time to swab my dog. Fortunately it was a pretty non-invasive process for him, and he was not really bothered by it at all. I don't think he even noticed I was doing anything! So that part was easy, fortunately.

2/27: When Orivet notified me that they had received my samples, the email included a link to check on the live status. It is not very useful - they say allow a 7-16 business days for results, and the live status tracker simply counts down from the maximum number of 16 business days. If it had, say, info about where they were at in the process, some tips/info/etc, anything - it would be much more useful.

3/23: Was not notified that my results were available, but found on the website that the results had been available since 3/16. There is a wealth of information available once your results are in. Aside from the detailed breed information, there is a lot of health information. Fortunately the testing indicated that my dog did not have any disease mutations in his DNA, but I was impressed with how many different things were tested for! I saw on a couple different screens a mention of how info could be sent to your vet, but it took a bit of clicking to find the vet results (Click on profile, then click on profile, and scroll down to "Veterinarian Report." This will open in a new window, and at the bottom, there is the option to generate a PDF.). I spent a lot of time clicking around and found where both of my barcodes were listed - apparently the results are only complete for one barcode, but they are pending for the other. Will update again when that one has finished! I'm very curious what extra information that will provide!

q.name: Super excited to use this. We were super excited when we got our ancestry DNA results. We figured th…

content.p: Super excited to use this. We were super excited when we got our ancestry DNA results. We figured this would be the perfect opportunity to do some background into our pups as well. ORIVET Dog DNA Test | Comprehensive Dog Breed Test Kit is a great product. Everything was incredibly simple to use. Instructions in the box were super clear and the set included a few extra swabs just in case the first sample collection didn’t go as planned. The website was very easy to use and we had our kit activated within a matter of a few minutes. Collection is a breeze with just a quick swab of the dogs cheeks. We are very much looking forward to finding the results from this. Very happy and easy to use products. Great!

q.name: Not user-friendly

content.p: Not user-friendly

q.name: Vine Customer Review of Free Product

content.p: Vine Customer Review of Free Product

q.name: This kit comes with two sets of two swabs in it. You would never guess from any of the instructions,…

content.p: This kit comes with two sets of two swabs in it. You would never guess from any of the instructions, or the photos of only one set of swabs on the listing, or almost any other part of the documentation or online presence of the company, but it does. There are two sets of two swabs, two swab envelopes, and two sets of random quantities of barcode stickers. One of the stickers is already stuck on one set of swabs, with matching stickers loose in the box. The others are inside the other swab envelope. There are no specific directions for what to do about any of this. There seem to be several extra barcode stickers, but it's never mentioned where they all go. One of the barcodes is the main kit barcode, and one isn't, but they don't tell you which is which. (Hint: One swab set is the "health screen collection kit." The main kit barcode is the one that goes with the OTHER set of swabs, that isn't labeled with anything telling you what it's for. I think.)

When you get to the website to try to activate the two sets of barcodes, the website doesn't tell you which is which but they DO differentiate between the two sets of codes. You have to use the main one from above as the first code, and the health code as the second. Otherwise you'll get an error.

They could have set this up so you just use one set of samples or one set of barcodes for two samples but instead it feels like they just added some new parts to the kit without updating any of their documentation or swab/barcode packaging. I wouldn't care about that so much if it were a cost-saving measure for the consumer but this is in the same price range as Embark. We ordered the Embark DNA kit on sale last year and it wasn't like this at all. It has some user-friendliness issues as well but there is ONE swab and one code for ALL the tests, which is significantly easier and less complicated. The packaging is clear and explicit, and the online resources answer almost any question you might have. There are videos showing how to use the swabs and how to increase your likelihood of getting a successful sample.

The Orivet kit comes with the four swabs, and they're like mascara wands (spoolies). They're abrasive and they have to be stood up (without contamination) to dry for 10 minutes before you can put them in the plastic-coated envelope to mail them back. We stood them up in a short coffee mug. We haven't finished the second set yet because our dog was big mad about the process. The Embark swab is a large piece of round foam on a plastic stick. Our dog didn't even care about that one at all. But she doesn't like having the inside of her mouth abraded by a spoolie, and she really doesn't want it done TWICE. Embark is way easier to use.

The Embark kit has a stabilizing fluid in a test tube that you put the swab in when you're done. You don't have to stand it up anywhere. It also has vastly superior instructions for every step of the process, although a lot of them are online rather than in the box where they'd be useful. I had concerns about mailing a sample out that hasn't been stabilized, but lots of companies do it this way so I guess it's probably not a big deal.

So far I'm really annoyed with this process. Even with the two sets of everything, this could have been significantly easier to use if they'd included comprehensive instructions and had a better support section on their website. Or even if they just had a photo of what's included in the box. It would take minimal effort on their part to make it easy for thousands of people to use. I even tried to look up YouTube videos and all that but there's just nothing helpful.
I'll update when we receive the results. I'll compare them with what we get from Embark. Maybe they saved money on the swabs and all that because their reports are so much better. I'm not sure.

content.p: Fun for my shelter dog!

q.name: Vine Customer Review of Free Product

content.p: Vine Customer Review of Free Product

q.name: I had no idea what breed(s) my foster dog was. He had fur like a poodle, was the size of a Newfie, b…

content.p: I had no idea what breed(s) my foster dog was. He had fur like a poodle, was the size of a Newfie, but the slender build of something like a lab! This test was easy to use on him to help flesh out what he was. The directions were clear and easy to follow. The swabs were easy to use; the hardest part was getting 100 pounds of dog to cooperate. You activate your kit before mailing it in and then results are emailed as they become available. It was really cool to see the dog's DNA breakdown by breed on the chart. He DID have some Newfie and poodle in there, and then 30% unknown, so he's a TRUE mutt! I was totally fine with that, and his adopted family really liked being able to see no risk for genetic diseases in him. I haven't done any other tests for pets like this, but this one seemed pretty neat to me. If you really need to know, this seems like a good tool to find out!

q.name: Vine Customer Review of Free Product

content.p: Vine Customer Review of Free Product

q.name: I had the opportunity to use this Orivet test after having used Wisdom Panel. Collecting the DNA sam…

content.p: I had the opportunity to use this Orivet test after having used Wisdom Panel. Collecting the DNA sample was easy and sending it in was also easy. I submitted my test at the start of the pandemic, so I assume results would be delayed. I received the health predisposition information back from Orivet quickly. The breed identification took much longer to get the results and I never received an email from Orivet to say that the results were ready, like I had with the health information. I had to periodically check my account and ended up forgetting to check until recently. by early June, the breed test was in and it matched Wisdom Panel exactly.

I thought it was odd that Wisdom Panel and Orivet would be exactly the same, right down to the percentages. My dog came back as Rottweiler, Chow, Shetland Sheepdog, and unknown. All of these breeds made sense to me. It was still weird that the results were the same as Wisdom Panel and that the breed identification test took so much longer to receive than the health information. I added a picture to my review of my dog and you can see that she looks very much like the breeds from her test results.
Given that Wisdom Panel yielded the same results, I did some digging on the Orivet website and buried in their partners section was Mars Veterinary. Mars Veterinary owns Wisdom Panel.

For the genetic testing for disease indicators, I can't find any information on whether Orivet runs these tests themselves. Their FAQ section is thick was jargon. Under the accreditation FAQ, they say that they hold a certificate to operate a QMS. It's assumed that the reader knows that QMS stands for "quality management system." Another question in the FAQ asks if the testing is run in an accredited facility and the answer is that "Orivet can offer accredited testing..." and mentions that their subcontractor meetings the requirements of A2LA R216.
On June 7, I wrote to Orivet to get more information about whether they ran any of their own tests and to understand their relationship with Mars Veterinary. As of June 13, I have not heard back.

The results of my test seem accurate and reasonable. The test did not find any predisposition to diseases causing mast cell tumors though, from which my dogs has suffered multiple occurrences. It's possible that this predisposition doesn't show up in genetic tests though or that it's not a genetic disease. Her test shows a predisposition to a specific fur and skin disposition, which made sense to me because she gets little crusty patches on her skin.

I think the Orivet test kit is good overall and I recommend it. I am unhappy with the obfuscation on their website on who actually runs their tests. Many people like to try different genetic tests for their mixed breed dogs. The fact that Orivet is not open about using the same labs as Wisdom Panel is frustrating. It took digging to figure that out and the only reason I knew that Mars Veterinary runs Wisdom Panel is because I have a friend who works for Mars. Unless a consumer does a lot of research ahead of time, the consumer won't know that they are buying the same breed detector test as Wisdom Panel. I'm still unsure whether they run their own test for the disease predispositions and I wish customer support had responded to me.
Overall, I'd look the price between Wisdom Panel and the Orivet test and pick the lower cost option. Do not waste your money buying both tests.

content.p: Just like when you do your own DNA test, this one comes back with the same types of results. It'll tell you the whole history of dog breeds that made up the genetics of your pup. It's so easy to use, and while test results to take a little time to come back, they were still far faster than when I sent in my own DNA to 23&Me and did my own DNA test. That took months to come back. This one just weeks. So if you're wanting to know the breed of dog you have, this is an easy way to find out!

q.name: AVOID! DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY First off, look at the photos from the other reviewers. On their resu…

content.p: AVOID! DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY

First off, look at the photos from the other reviewers. On their result photos, it shows that their dog is basically CLEAR of all disease mutations. My dog has dental issues, seizures, respiratory, and joint issues. So why did she test clear of every mutation?

There’s the longstanding perception that mixed-breed dogs are less likely than purebred dogs to develop disease. While this may be true, a study published on PLoS Genetics (“Frequency and distribution of 152 genetic disease variants in over 100,000 mixed breed and purebred dogs”) shows that despite mixed-breed dogs being less disease-prone than purebred dogs, they are more likely than purebred dogs to be carriers of the recessive disorders studied. So why is that our results are being reported with not a single “CARRIER” hit, despite supposedly testing for over 200 genetic diseases and traits? So, I’m calling this test out for the scam that it is.

The health risks portion is a joke as well. They basically pull up the breed(s) + age your pet tested for and generate Wiki-info that is free to Google, and definitely not worth the $169 price tag that the test costs. So since your pet didn’t test for any “RISK”/”CARRIER” option, you’re basically going off Wiki info of what COULD happen to your pet, despite paying $169 to find out if it WILL. Basically, you paid $169 for an over glorified breed test that you can get for a fraction of the cost. The life plan portion is also not a worth- while upgrade since it's basically an online health journal.
Avoid this and go with Embark or something.

q.name: The swabbing part was easy, you just rub it on the inside of your dogs cheeks. Then mail in the prep…

content.p: The swabbing part was easy, you just rub it on the inside of your dogs cheeks. Then mail in the prepaid envelope. It took several weeks before I got an email that is was received. But that was the last I heard from them. I had to just keep checking my account on the website. Low and behold about a month or so later, my results were there. My dog came back as just a 50/50 mix of two breeds. Based on what my dog looks like it seems pretty accurate. Definitely looks more like one than the other. But with DNA you never know what traits are going to come out more in a mixed dog. My dog came out completely clear on all the other tests. I assume it's correct, but without running the same test with another company, I guess I won't know. It's been over a month now since I discovered that my results were available, and I still was never notified by the company. You just have to keep checking your account I guess.

q.name: Ordered this to test my Rat terrier. I already know the his breed but not sure if there are others (…

content.p: Ordered this to test my Rat terrier. I already know the his breed but not sure if there are others (supposed to be a purebred). Reading the documentation included with the test it is apparent that your results will only be an educated guess. My assumption is that there is very little known research on dog DNA so educated guess is about all you can expect. The bonus is information on genetic defects and potential health problems. My pup is prone to many health and genetic issues being a purebred.

The test is simple enough, pinch the dogs cheek and swab the inside for 15 seconds. For most house pets this should be simple enough but my pup did not like anything jammed into his mouth so trying to collect the samples proved to be more difficult than expected. While he did eventually let me collect from him it was a battle to get him calm enough to let me touch his face. Another thing to keep in mind is that they shouldn't have eaten at least 15 minutes before the samples are collected. On the first collection attempt I was bribing him with treats, then I finished reading the instruction. I was able to collect the samples late at night before bed while he slept on my lap so it eventually worked out.

q.name: It was easy to use with cool results. Customer service was fantastic. We forgot to register the numb…

content.p: It was easy to use with cool results. Customer service was fantastic. We forgot to register the numbers and they helped us locate it and register. The results were pretty nice and to know my dog is healthy even with conditions larger dogs maybe prone to.

q.name: Vine Customer Review of Free Product

content.p: Vine Customer Review of Free Product

q.name: The product needs serious help with their website. Although the test shows "resulted," I can not acc…

content.p: The product needs serious help with their website. Although the test shows "resulted," I can not access the results. I emailed customer service who said the results are done and
"should be available soon" (but they are not.)

The website lacks basic functionality. For example, there is a section to input your dog's birthdate and weight, there is an option to select "pounds or kilograms"--but it defaults to kilograms and won't let you select pounds.
This is basic broken website functions and something that could be easily fixed, but they don't seem to care. They are aware of the problem, but brushed me off on that too.

There is no alert or email that your results are ready. You must keep checking back with the website and even after it says "resulted" --you don't know what the results are. This seems like an overpriced product compared to similar products with better website navigation and customer information.

**Updated** so I am revising my 2 star review to 4 stars. I FINALLY got my results and I will say this: The company is accurate. I submitted two different tests to Orivet on the same dog using different email addresses and different dog names. (Both tests were for the same dog submitted two months apart.) The breed results were identical even though I submitted a photo for the first test and no photo for the second test. So they are not guessing off photos here. The second test included 150+ DNA health screening for which our mutt came up with no adverse health markers (this was a relief)

I debated on 3 stars or 4 based on their horrible website. They REALLY need to work on it. My dog is female and small and they estimate her full grown weight at 68lbs. There is no way this is remotely accurate. Even the vet estimates she will be 45lbs full grown. We have a second dog who is 62lbs and this puppy has been much smaller at all stages of growth. The website is difficult to navigate and to change basic info (she is growing, so her weight is changing and I really wanted a weight estimator that changed as she grew based on age.

There are mistakes on the website such as them asking for my dog's gender (intact female) and referring to her as "him" throughout the report. There also should be a "printer friendly" full report that you can press "print here" and print the entire report for your vet. There isn't --it is all convoluted into sections that make no sense. Don't make us navigate through all this, just give us a full printable report with all the info.
I actually hope they make some changes and allow me to review an updated version. I think the information they provide is useful, but I do hope they take the changes into consideration.

q.name: I bought two of these for my two cats. The results were gobblety-gook (see attached) and then, on th…

content.p: I bought two of these for my two cats. The results were gobblety-gook (see attached) and then, on the “profile” the results referred to my cat as a “dog” (obviously, cats were an after-thought to their template). What is sad is that this is a great concept (dna for cats) and takes only minimal marketing effort to tailor to cat-owners, but this company did nothing special. I sent two complaints as in my two web profiles, the data kept referring to my “dogs” with dog-specific characteristics. The actual results provided no useful info at all. I email two complaints and never heard back (there is no customer service). Please don’t spend your $100 on this outfit. They cannot deliver on cat dna product or service. Scam.

q.name: I have a cat who has a disorder that causes significant physical disability and appears to be a gene…

content.p: I have a cat who has a disorder that causes significant physical disability and appears to be a genetic disorder. It is one Basepaws screens for (she meets the phenotype for it- she has all of the signs and symptoms). This is why I did the test- to confirm she has it.

Unfortunately, cat genetics are much more poorly understood than human or even dog genetics. She tested negative for the singular mutation that has been identified in cats (100+ mutations to a single gene can cause it in humans!). The vet geneticist asked us to try testing her again to confirm before sending her DNA in for research so we will do that.

I know a lot of people use this test to find out what “breed” their cat is. And I’m concerned because most cats simply do not have a breed, and many cat breeds simply do not have enough that genetically distinguishes them from other breeds to be accurately identified. And while it’s in the fine print, the company doesn’t claim to identify breed, but rather see which breed your cat is most like genetically.

So I think this is probably a perfectly fine test if you want to screen for genetic disorders, with the understanding that cat genetics is just much more poorly understood, or want to amuse yourself with the breed reports!
I think when the family member finding feature is unveiled this will be a much more interesting test!

q.name: Super fun way to pass some days in quarantine!

content.p: Super fun way to pass some days in quarantine!

q.name: With every single day strongly resembling the previous, we decided we needed something to look forwa…

content.p: With every single day strongly resembling the previous, we decided we needed something to look forward to this summer. So my 12 year old daughter and I wrangled up the shelter cat we adopted and sent in his sample.

We talked about what he might be every day, it was SO thrilling to get the emails at each stage of the process. And when we finally got the results, it was wonderful to pour over them and to see that they were spot on for his personality and looks. A huge bonus, in my opinion, is having the health screen. We know now what to watch out for in the future with our beloved cat.
Overall impressions:
Super easy and fast to order the kit
User-friendly instructions of how to collect the sample
Great communication at every stage of the process (very much like 23 and Me)
Seemingly highly accurate results based on the personality and appearance of the cat
A super great way to have something to talk about and look forward to with your kids during this long summer

content.p: My Scarlet was dropped off at the local animal shelter at 4 weeks old with no momma only a sibling. We wanted to know what type of breed they were as they communicate with lots of vocalizations. Our test did not disappoint and confirmed some of their breed, part Abyssinian. Never would of thought Maine coon but they are 1/3. I highly recommend using Basepaws as it’s easy to use, they send you updates throughout the process.

q.name: Smooth process & quick turn around time

content.p: Smooth process & quick turn around time

q.name: Kit arrives in a few days after purchasing & has a quick turn around time since swabbing the sample…

content.p: Kit arrives in a few days after purchasing & has a quick turn around time since swabbing the sample can be done in one day & mailed back out! You also receive emails every step of the way to fill you in on the status of your kit: when your kit arrives at the facilities, when the dna has been successfully extracted from sample, when dna is sent to be sequenced & one last email saying they are analyzing the data they’ve collected. Loved it, never had me guessing when I should expect to receive his results. The total time for me was approximately 4 weeks, fitting well within the 4-6 week turn around guarantee! The breed + health marker reports in generally have been super helpful as my cat is a rescue of the streets with FIV. It gives me peace of mind knowing he is not as risk for any major health diseases, especially since they would be more difficult for him to fight off with FIV. Overall super beneficial and worth the money if you’re able to purchase. Especially since your cats results will update/expand over time as more join the program. Hoping one of my cat’s siblings joins the program one day so we can connect! Would recommend to others!

q.name: Living in the country I have a variety of feral cats that temporarily live in my barn and raise thei…

content.p: Living in the country I have a variety of feral cats that temporarily live in my barn and raise their litters of kittens. I couldn’t pass up a couple of beauties and decided to bring them into a domestic lifestyle. I was so curious what breeds they were so decided to get 1 kit to test one of the kitties who was identical to her brother. It was so cool to see the variety of breeds present in my “wild” kitty. After a friend wanted me to capture two more kittens from a different breed, I got attached and couldn’t give them up! I decided to get two more kits to see if there were similarities to my first kitty from a different litter (same mom). While some rare similarities were there, I was shocked to see a wider array of breeds in one kitty (Maine Coone and Peterbald)! Very, very cool! I’m excited to see what else will come of the research BasePaws continues to find! I love this product!

q.name: Lots of great info!

content.p: Lots of great info!

q.name: I adopted a cat about six years ago and had no idea what she was. She's long haired, fairly small, a…

content.p: I adopted a cat about six years ago and had no idea what she was. She's long haired, fairly small, and has short legs. We couldn't narrow down what she might be so I decided to do a DNA test to figure it out. She's had dental issues and doesn't like her mouth area touched so it was a little tricky to get the swab in her mouth but we managed. From receiving the kit to receiving the results it was about 5-6 weeks, which is what they advertise. It says she's mostly rag doll, maine coon, and american short hair. The breed results aren't super narrowed down, but that's what I expected. As more people send in their cat's samples, the database grows, and everyone's reports gets updated with more accurate info. There's also a lot of information about each breed's behavior, origins, and any health conditions they may be more susceptible to. Pretty much all of the info is accurate to her and I'm excited to get updates as their database grows! Totally recommend if you're curious like me!

q.name: As easy as a 23andme test

content.p: As easy as a 23andme test

q.name: I adopted a kitten from the shelter a few months ago and wanted to find out its breed and health cha…

content.p: I adopted a kitten from the shelter a few months ago and wanted to find out its breed and health characteristics. I felt like the kitten had all the characteristics of a russian blue but couldn't be sure unless I got the test. Luckily around thanksgiving, the breed and health test was on sale so I took the plunge.

The test was just like any standard mouth swab dna test. I was supposed to swab the inner cheek of the kitten for at least 4 to 5 seconds. However cats usually don't like that so I was only able to do it for a second before he tried squirming away. So I repeated the process a few times just to be safe that I got a good sample.

I closed it up and send it back in the prepaid box. A week later, my sample was received and the swab had enough genetic material to be sequenced. Now came the long task of waiting for the results. Normally it said to wait 4 to 6 weeks from sequencing start to report generated. I started checking the website every other day excited to see if perhaps they would be available sooner. True to their FAQ, I got my results back in about 4 weeks after they started sequencing. It was actually pretty impressive since a week of that time fell over the holidays.

Now the results did confirm some percentage of russian blue heritage but showcased a much higher percentage of ragdoll and siamese. The ragdoll and russian blue mix may make some sense but the siamese didn't really make sense to me as my cat had almost none of the characteristics listed. Ragdoll didn't make that much sense either but I guess that since the sample set for ragdoll is so high, the data would skew more towards ragdoll. Luckily no major health issues were reported.
Overall I rate this 4 stars while I wait for more owners to send their cat's dna in such that the report will get continuously updated with new accurate information.

q.name: Accurate & Informative DNA Dog Test

content.p: Accurate & Informative DNA Dog Test

q.name: I researched DNA test for dogs and found that DNAmydog offered the age test in combination with the…

content.p: I researched DNA test for dogs and found that DNAmydog offered the age test in combination with the breed. I was thrilled to know that my dog’s genetic age was a year younger than what two vets claimed her to be. Bella is a shelter dog and the more information I have of her, the better I can care for her. Bella is a pit mix & the CS team at DNAmydog was a great help explaining that the American Pitbull Terrier is not a recognized breed yet a combination of terrier and bully mixes. I was surprised to find that Bella had a little bit of German shorthaired pointer in her! I was very happy with the science & accuracy of the genetic testing and breed testing. I would recommend getting this to anyone who cares about the health of their dog. Great value for the dual test.

content.p: Disappointing to say the least... how my 25# wired haired stubby legged dog comes back as a level 3(20-30%) beagle, border collie, weimaraner mix is beyond me. I even supplied a photo but very little info and yall still came back with bogus results. Coworker raved about this dna kit. Should have went with my gut after seeing the reviews. Lesson learned.

q.name: They said it was likely our sample was contaminated by oral bacteria from another dog....we have an…

content.p: They said it was likely our sample was contaminated by oral bacteria from another dog....we have an 11 lb terrier mix, clearly terrier. We think Jack Russell (Its very very evident) and some other terrier like a yorkshire or minitaure poodle. The test returned three breeds, none as a Majority % match. Rottweiler, Viszla and Beagle. The beagle being the smallest of the three is still 2-3X larger than our dog. They are sending a replacement test to try again we'll see what the results say and I'll update accordingly. For now whatever STRs and SNPs they are using don't seem to be super accurate.

Also, the genetic age part of the test was interesting to see, it roughly corresponds to our guesses. Since I did not provide our dog picture or the age, which we don't know, the results are good.

q.name: I purchased the Base Paws test kit for my cat. The kit arrived as expected, and was very easy to use…

content.p: I purchased the Base Paws test kit for my cat. The kit arrived as expected, and was very easy to use.
The cat did not love getting her cheek swabbed, but it was a quick and painless process and she was fine afterward.
I mailed in the kit and a few weeks later we got the results!

q.name: Great Learning Tool!

content.p: Great Learning Tool!

q.name: I’ve gotten three of these kits for my cats throughout the years and have never had any problems wit…

content.p: I’ve gotten three of these kits for my cats throughout the years and have never had any problems with them! They are a great way to learn about your cat’s personality traits and even get a jumpstart on managing any potential future health problems.

I will say that one of my kitties had severe early-onset HCM (diagnosed at 5 months and died at only 1.5 years old) and the health report didn’t catch those genes so there is still some progress still to be done. The more you buy and contribute the more research is done and the more illnesses that can be caught!
I love basepaws!

q.name: Solving Life's Greatest Mysteries

content.p: Solving Life's Greatest Mysteries

q.name: My partner bought this test as a solution to our ongoing debate of "is he fat, or just a Norwegian F…

content.p: My partner bought this test as a solution to our ongoing debate of "is he fat, or just a Norwegian Forest Cat?" Turns out he was 1.04% right. The cheek swab was not difficult for us but it was a two person job.
You can adjust the results from a range of "maybe" to "confident" thought the variance between those should narrow as they do more tests.
I liked the health markers, too. Cats are notoriously bad at letting their owners know when something is wrong, so I feel comfortable knowing that he is not genetically predisposed to any diseases.
Also it was very exciting to change his profile at the very from "domestic long hair" to "Maine coon."

q.name: Highly Recommend BasePaws

content.p: Highly Recommend BasePaws

content.p: Excited to find a way to research breed and genetics with my cat. BasePaws was extremely easy to use and the report comprehensive. I'm looking forward to sharing the report with the Veterinarian to help in long term care of my cat.

q.name: Excellent customer service!

content.p: Excellent customer service!

q.name: The company was amazing. When I lost my first kit in the mail they sent me another one free of charg…

content.p: The company was amazing. When I lost my first kit in the mail they sent me another one free of charge! They also sent me lots of updates about where we were at in the dna process.

I bought this for my cat on her birthday -she is from a shelter - because she has had some health concerns (and has the softest rabbit like coat) and I heard some great things about the product online.

I noticed other reviews discuss the validity of the report. And I want to mention there a slider that allows you to see the confident analysis of your cats breed all the way to the less probable but still very fun to look at possible breeds that your cat can be. I felt that the results were accurate as they match my cats personality and traits (soft fur).

q.name: Worth every penny

content.p: Worth every penny

q.name: I bought two Breed + Health DNA kits as a Christmas gift for my wife. She had no idea such a thing e…

content.p: I bought two Breed + Health DNA kits as a Christmas gift for my wife. She had no idea such a thing existed, so she was thrilled to receive the gift, especially since she always wondered about their breeds. The depth and quality of the report is fantastic. The cats are a little bit of a lot of things, as expected, but still give us more understanding than we had before. The health markers alone makes this product worth purchase. We understood ahead of time that this information could be negative and stressful, but still valuable, as there are preventive action items we could take now depending on the results. I was definitely nervous about receiving "bad" news about our beloved cats on the holiday, but that was not the case. The similarity to wild cats things was mostly filler but still cute and fun; my daughter absolutely loves that our cat "is like" a tiger and the other a cheetah. One final point- I submitted the results for both cats on the same day, but the reports came back 23 days apart. This was no big deal, and I assume due to Covid. Worth mentioning in case anyone else is concerned that they haven't received all of their reports at once. Be patient :)

q.name: If your hesitant just DO IT!!! Such a good experience with BasePaws.

q.name: This DNA test exceeded my expectations. The whole process was so easy and honestly fun. They keep yo…

content.p: This DNA test exceeded my expectations. The whole process was so easy and honestly fun. They keep you updated the whole time and I really feel like they go above and beyond to explain everything to you. I was worried that Winston had stomach issues and it was very relieving to me that everything came back clear for him. Reading about his breed groups was also very fascinating to me! I want to get another one for our Persian girl Fergie to compare Winston’s results to hers. Follow us on Instagram @sirwinston_ladyferg. We hope to share more of our Basepaws journey there!

q.name: I have been very pleased with my basepaws experience. I have no knowledge of my kitty's father and t…

content.p: I have been very pleased with my basepaws experience. I have no knowledge of my kitty's father and the vet just classified my cat, Mousecop, as black tabby. He started to grow really fast, was very talkative and very smart and from what I read I thought he may have Bengal in him. his results showed a confident match to ragdoll, Maine Coon and Savanah cat! when I read the traits of these cats it was Mousecop spot on. I was also surprised at the amount of health markers that were tested and that he was only a carrier of retinal degeneration! the wait to get results was not long at all and the swab to collect saliva was easy to use.

I am happy with the data the test was able to provide from genetic makeup to health concerns, it was all interesting and easy to read. Everything was explained in terms that were understandable because a lot of the time science can get a little overwhelming! Overall, loved my experience and would recommend to anyone wanting to learn more about their furry feline child

q.name: Basepaws was a great experience for me and my cat Lexi. (I bought directly from their website during…

content.p: Basepaws was a great experience for me and my cat Lexi. (I bought directly from their website during a sale) it took about 5-6 weeks in total to receive the report from the day I sent in the kit.

I had suspected for a little while that my cat was not Siamese because she had longer fur than I expected a Siamese to. Basepaws confirmed my suspicion as she is in fact part ragdoll and Himalayan. Made perfect sense. While their database is not yet complete they definitely have a growing range of breeds in there (I had no idea what a peterbald was) and they provide a range of certainty on the breeds found in the cats DNA.The genetic health screening was also great as I got to confirm that she is not predisposed to any health conditions.

content.p: I ordered this because my cat was a rescue and I was told he was part Maine coon. I was super interested in knowing more about his DNA and it turns out he’s quite the mix! While he does have Maine coon dna, he’s also quite a bit Ragdoll! I can see it in his face! It was super cool to see what parts of his personality are attributed to what parts of his dna too. And I’m super happy to know he has know genetic health markers!

q.name: Although I won't be breeding my cat, it is good to know that he is a carrier for Factor XII deficien…

content.p: Although I won't be breeding my cat, it is good to know that he is a carrier for Factor XII deficiency. I'd highly recommend Basepaws to anyone who loves their feline friend(s) - Basepaws frequently updates our reports with new findings, more accurate results, and new features. Once you purchase a kit, you also get added to an amazing FB community of fellow cat lovers where we share pics, videos, tips, and questions. Overall, the value of Basepaws pays for itself - you can lifetime updates as they get more samples into their database (hoping for a future where cat genetics and breeds are just as well understood as dogs!) so you are also helping out the community/research with each purchase. 10/10 would recommend

q.name: Results Are Questionable

content.p: Results Are Questionable

q.name: I purchased 5 Breed + Health Report kits. I sent in the kits for my calico & orange tabby at the sam…

content.p: I purchased 5 Breed + Health Report kits. I sent in the kits for my calico & orange tabby at the same time, but the results didn't seem to match the cats, so I wondered if the kits or results were accidentally switched at the BasePaws facility. I sent in a 2nd kit on the orange tabby, but the results didn't match either of the tests originally submitted.

While waiting for the results of the orange tabby's "re-test", I sent in the kit for my siamese. There was a problem with the sample and BasePaws sent a replacement kit for a re-test on the siamese, except the replacement kit was a less-expensive "Breed Only" kit, not the Breed + Health Report kit I had purchased & submitted. Also while still waiting for the results of the re-test on the orange tabby, I submitted the 5th of my original 5 kits for a gray striped tabby. After receiving the results of the contradictory orange tabby re-test, I doubt the results of any of the 5 submissions on the 4 cats. Money down the drain. I can't recommend BasePaws Breed Analysis Kits since the results are questionable. The accuracy of any test is dependent upon the human who is administering and analyzing the results.

content.p: While I did enjoy doing the test on my cat, I think it’s more or less pretty vague. My cat was almost 59% unknown. This isn’t the fault of the test, the bank for cats isn’t large enough yet. I have a long haired polydactyl who looks Norwegian Forest/ Maine Coon. He showed dna for Russian Blue, America shorthair, Maine Coon and Ragdoll.

I didn’t know much about the testing before doing it so I was expecting to find out a lot more, that will come years down the road as the bank expands. The company is available for questions, and they have a Facebook group where you can connect with others who have done the test.

q.name: Adopted my new fur ball after my precious cat left me for the other side of the rainbow. My new cat…

content.p: Adopted my new fur ball after my precious cat left me for the other side of the rainbow. My new cat looked much like a Siamese cat, maybe with a mixture of some other breeds. The adoption papers just said white cat, I knew better but needed proof to confirm my belief. What better way than using Basepaws DNA kit to get results to confirm my cats breeds and as a bonus get information regarding my cats health. After mailing in the easy to use sample kit, I registered online and within a short time received an emailed that my sample was received. Within a reasonable amount of time through another email, I was notified the DNA analysis was complete. The information is presented on-line and is easy to understand using nice graphics. Now I know more about my cats genetics and health than I thought possible. What I like is as the database continues to expand, my cats genetics will be updated as more information becomes available through additional DNA testing, same as the human DNA database. I am a happy customer.

q.name: So, I've previously used 23andMe on myself (a human). I bought the Breed + Health kit for my two cat…

content.p: So, I've previously used 23andMe on myself (a human). I bought the Breed + Health kit for my two cats. Getting the sample from the cats was easier than expected, just a quick swap of their cheek. They didn't like it, but they didn't put up too much of a fuss. What's more important are that I got results back of their breed make-up. Keep in mind that my two cats are rescues, so I have no real information I have about them is limited. But now, I know their breed makeup and their personality types based on breed. Some of it is very true!

q.name: This dna test is simple to use. It comes ready to use in a sterile package, you just swab the inside…

content.p: This dna test is simple to use. It comes ready to use in a sterile package, you just swab the inside cheek of kitty to collect some saliva. Put the swab in the tube and seal it up and send it in. It doesn't hurt kitty and it's great finding out heritage. My favorite part is knowing my kitties are clear for health issues as they have no markers for them. This is a wonderful product and a great community of cat lovers!

q.name: Breed reports need to be improved.

content.p: Breed reports need to be improved.

q.name: So I got this cat DNA kit; because Basepaws finally added Health markers, this year. The health repo…

content.p: So I got this cat DNA kit; because Basepaws finally added Health markers, this year. The health reports were straight forward and mostly easy to understand.
The Breed reports were more vague; and I had to watch some videos, and a hard to find new Basepaws help article.
From how I understand it, Basepaws currently believes my cat has...

39.5% Western DNA; and shares DNA with all 7 breeds in that group. The American Shorthair was assigned top spot, because dominant genes or physical looks-wise my cat got his tabby stripes from this breed.
He has 17.01% Eastern DNA; and shares DNA with all four breeds in that group.

He seems to have the American Burmese's small skull and short nose muzzle. The American Burmese is short legged, and the European Burmese is long legged. My cat has short front legs and tall back hind legs.

He has 7.77% Exotic DNA; and shares DNA with all 3 breeds in that group. The Egyptian Mau has short front legs and tall back hind legs. The Egyptian Mau is the fastest domestic cat; and wiggle vibrates it's tail to mark it's territory.

He has 12% Persian DNA; and shares DNA with all 4 breeds in that group. The Exotic Shorthair comes in a wide range of colors; has shaggy short fur; and holds it's tail low to the ground, most of the time.
In short, these top four breeds are where my cat gets all his physical traits. So I think Basepaws's breed analysis is good, but could be presented better and clearer.
I would like Basepaws to add a traits list, so I don't have to do the research on any future cats I might get. Cat blood type would be nice. Sibling and parent relationships as well.
The Polycat DNA is unknown DNA, that might be whittled down in a couple years.

q.name: Happy with the test

content.p: Happy with the test

q.name: Pretty easy to use! I was pretty happy with the results. I adopted my cat three years ago and it’s n…

content.p: Pretty easy to use! I was pretty happy with the results. I adopted my cat three years ago and it’s nice to know a little of his background and where he came from. Though my my cat would be Maine coon but he’s actually mostly similar to Norwegian forest cat. I can totally see that! Either way, I love him unconditionally. It was a relief that he was clear from being carrier of all the diseases in their database. Yay! My fire baby is healthy!’

One of my babies came from a shelter and I wanted to know not just what his ancestry was but also if he was predisposed to any kind of genetic diseases. My other baby came from a friends litter so I knew about his mom and dad but again wanted to make sure he was gonna healthy kitty.
If you want to know about your fur babies I would wholly recommend basepaw!

q.name: I loved the report! It was really fun! I found out my six month old kitten's genetics which totally…

content.p: I loved the report! It was really fun! I found out my six month old kitten's genetics which totally makes sense with the way he looks and his little personality. Of course he did not like getting his little cheek swabbed but it was totally worth it. I think one of the best things about the report is the genetic markers for medical. He didn't have any positives so I was relieved and hope he'll be healthy and happy for a long time to come. He looks like a little mini Panther but is closest to the Cheetah for big cats. He also has some Bengal in his dna which might explain why he is so huge already compared to my other cat. He is most like the Russian Blue, with Oriental Shorthair next, followed by Bengal and British Shorthair last. This was a really neat report and I really enjoyed it!

q.name: I have two wonderful kitties, one is a beautiful sleek panther and one is a lush fuzzy brown long-ha…

content.p: I have two wonderful kitties, one is a beautiful sleek panther and one is a lush fuzzy brown long-haired tabby. They were both rescues so I had absolutely zero idea what type of cats they were. I've had some vets tell me that my long-haired boy is "definitely" Maine Coon but he didn't quite have the face so I've always been curious as to what he is. My DNA tests came back and showed that both of my kitties are a unique blend of so many wonderful cats. Most importantly, the health screens showed that they're not currently carriers or at risk for any of the diseases they test for - which is great for my anxious self. I would definitely recommend this for anyone curious about their kitties!

The ancestral info was super neat and made perfect sense for our kitten. The genetic testing was very informative and helpful in knowing what he's predisposed to and what he is a carrier for. Only negative was the test took a while but they send emails along the way so you are completely informed about what is going on. I like not being left in the dark for weeks. Totally recommend. We're getting another for our other rescue cat.

q.name: I chose Embark because of their larger breed database and the health screenings they offered. Yes it…

content.p: I chose Embark because of their larger breed database and the health screenings they offered. Yes it cost more, but totally worth it. Collecting the sample was challenging as my dog only cooperated for about 28 seconds. You are supposed to collect for 30-60 seconds. I was so afraid they wouldn't have enough. They communicated with me through the whole process so I knew what was happening. The health screening was very detailed and extensive. The breed report was so cool. He is a rescue and was supposed to be a Chihuahua. As he grew, we knew he was much more but had no idea some of the breeds he had in him. Absolutely worth every penny!

I was extremely happy with the health information I received. We were aware of this health issue prior to the testing so we knew the test was accurate. This condition isn't common in his breed.
I love the fact that they have partnered with Cornell.
Also, I love seeing my dog's relatives and being able to connect with them.

q.name: Embark is the real deal - highly recommend

content.p: Embark is the real deal - highly recommend

q.name: Our puppy was dumped at a shelter with his mom and nine siblings. We knew his mom was a German Sheph…

content.p: Our puppy was dumped at a shelter with his mom and nine siblings. We knew his mom was a German Shepherd, but we didn’t know what his dad was, and we were so curious. Our vet recommended Embark and we ordered the kit. It was very easy to use the swab and send off for the results. It took a few weeks to get the results back, and we received awesome email updates while we waited.

I have to be honest, I was a little skeptical about the whole thing. When I was filling out the information, I purposely left off his breed info because I didn’t want to give any clues as to what he was. So I left the breed part blank and didn’t include any pictures of our puppy. Embark had no info other than his name, and city where we adopted him.

I was shocked when the results came back with 50% German Shepherd. Seriously shocked. They were right on the money! We were thrilled with the results and so happy to know what the other 50% of our pup was. Now we know. Thank you Embark!

q.name: Very Accurate With Results to Prove It!!!

q.name: I am extremely happy with the service. I adopted Sophie from my niece who works for the Aggieland Hu…

content.p: I am extremely happy with the service. I adopted Sophie from my niece who works for the Aggieland Humane Society in College Station, Texas. My niece also fostered Sophie's five puppies (all of her puppies were adopted before her). When Sophie's results came in she was matched with one of her puppies as a direct relative. We had no idea that her puppy's family had even done a DNA test. It was pretty awesome and truly affirmed how accurate embark really is!

It would be nice to have a better way to connect with potential canine relatives

I would recommend before you swab, to wash their mouth with clean water, wait the time recommended then swab for a cleaner dna result.

q.name: I would recommend embark to anyone with a furry friend! It helps you learn more about them in order…

content.p: I would recommend embark to anyone with a furry friend! It helps you learn more about them in order to give them the best lifestyle and keep them healthy. You also get to connect with people who have a close genetic match with your dog. I have a pure 100% Siberian Husky and I was able to connect with someone else who got their husky from the same breeder! The app also lets you organize your dogs: health, breed info, research, close relatives, traits, etc. Overall, embark just gives you so much insight on your dogs health and breed and that’s why I think it’s worth it to know their genetics.

I received the test a day after I ordered it, swabbed my dogs cheek and mailed it in.. Every step of the way embark emailed me status of the DNA testing process. A few weeks later they email me with result. The whole process was quick and easy, in the information was very detailed. I also found that my pup has a brother a few miles to the south of us

Loved getting all of the updates throughout the process and glad they identify between different bully breeds unlike some other companies! Definitely will use again with my next pup.

q.name: I felt like a kid on Christmas!

content.p: I felt like a kid on Christmas!

q.name: If you're thinking about doing this and you have the money, DO IT. I had so much fun obsessing over…

content.p: If you're thinking about doing this and you have the money, DO IT. I had so much fun obsessing over Rosie's results and sharing and talking about them with our friends and family!

I got Rosie on Craigslist from someone who believed her to be a "lab/border collie mix". I wasn't concerned about her breed at all but the more she grew out of the puppy stage the more obvious it became that she was definitely not a lab or a border collie! My fiance got me the breed and health kit for Christmas and I was so so excited.

It takes a few weeks to get the results but it's well worth the wait. They send you emails to update you about where they are in the process. The website is super easy to navigate and overall I think Embark just has a good system going.

When I got Rosie's results I was so excited and I rode that high for like three days! It was just so so fun getting to see the explanation of why she's so freaking cute and learning more about her. I'm obsessed.
I am super satisfied with the amount of information I received. It was fun to see other dogs with similar breed mixes, and we even found a couple of Rosie's relatives!
I would absolutely recommend this to anyone who is thinking of doing it.

I got the breed+health kit, but if I were doing it again, I would have just done the breed kit. Rosie doesn't really have any health concerns and I'm a broke young adult, so it just wasn't super worth it to me. But if you want to have that information about your pet's health and you can afford it, go for it!

content.p: Most of the time when you adopt a rescued dog you know nothing about your new family member. It was so wonderful and a surprise to find out what breeds Edie is made up of. Knowing these breeds has helped us understand why she has some behaviors that she has. We didn’t know that her pushing us with her nose and open mouth on the backs of our calves was a herding behavior that can be found in the Australian shepherds, which she is 50% of. We found a family member too!!! We haven’t contacted them, but did find it enjoyable to see how different they look from Edie. The genetic information was helpful as well. It was good to know that Edie doesn’t have any issues to be concerned about. Embark has also helped us with providing a path to look into for addressing Edie’s reactivity to other dogs.I would totally do this again and would recommend this to family and friends. This was a fun and enlightening experience!

q.name: Took 3 weeks to get results, but they did mention it may take longer due to holiday rush. They email…

content.p: Took 3 weeks to get results, but they did mention it may take longer due to holiday rush. They emailing me updates every few days, I appreciated the contact.

Product was very easy to use, and had well laid out steps to follow. Nothing confusing about the packaging. I do recommend waving a dog treat around before collecting the saliva. I tried at first with no dog treat and my dogs mouth was as dry as the desert. Just make sure you put the treat up high so they don't grab it.

The website is easy to use and navigate. The interface is very informative and interesting, they try to explain everything as easily as possible. Going so far as to explain why a dog may have a certain breeds color coat without actually being at all related to that dog breed. Genetics is fascinating and complicated. Most of the bad reviews seem to be related to people upset that their dog isn't a certain breed, if you approach this test with an openness and understanding of genetics you will be very happy. For instance, if your pure bred Duck Retriever comes out as a mix of Collie, Retriever and Shepard, you shouldn't be surprised, as that breed is created by crosses these other breeds. That is why it is important to mark on the test, when asked, if you dog is pure bred. Obviously, every breed of dog is created from another breed, and if your breed of dog is a recent invention, well, it most likely won't show up. I know some people are "testing" the DNA testers by not marking that their dog is pure bred, because they want to see if the lab will get it right. But you are just running the risk of the lab identifying the older breeds used to create your dog breed, and nothing more, talk about setting yourself up to fail, just tick the box.

I must admit I did not expect the health side of this test to be surprising, but it was. I got some very bad news about some chronic diseases which may pop up later, thanks to his pure bred Shepard side. But, now that I know what to look for I can act quicker and hopefully improve my dogs quality of life. I will always be grateful to this test for that.

As for the breed part of it, I was totally wrong! The pound told me over and over Hank was a Chow Chow mix. I watched many videos on training Chow Chows, approached all situations like he WAS a Chow Chow. He looks very much like a Chow Chow mix, my whole family had money on what other breeds were mixed WITH Chow Chow to make my dog. He doesn't have a single drop of Chow Chow in him! Thankfully I owe no one money as everyone in my family was wrong. I would have never guessed German Shepard, but I suppose I should have known, training him is a dream and he is the most loyal dog I have ever owned.
I will be doing this for all my future dogs! Very recommend.

Even though we're in New Jersey and the dog was found in Texas, we were even able to contact her siblings' families and confirm they are all from Texas born around the same time. They all even have the same strange bark that cannot be explained by her breed.

content.p: This company says 3 weeks from when they receive the kits you get the results. That was the case with one of our dogs but the other we are still waiting on. The website didn't accept my registration initially so I had to do that by email. These kits are quite expensive so I guess I had higher hopes. If you have a lot of extra time to contact them and don't mind waiting longer than the promised time, it seems to be a good test kit. Can't say I recommend though.
Update: Also, we finally got our other dog's results back and it was 37.5% unknown.

q.name: Don't buy this Orivet product - use any other DNA test other than these. The results provide absolut…

content.p: Don't buy this Orivet product - use any other DNA test other than these. The results provide absolutely no information and took 8+weeks. We purchased these for all of our dogs (8) and so far have only received 1 result back that was completely inaccurate. An AKC / CKC registered Standard Poodle with 6 generations documented genealogy.... Orivet shows one of the Great Grand Parents being an "unknown mixed breed". The Genetic health screen is far from what they promise on the packaging or their site and only provides insight into if they have potential for liver or adrenal gland problems... no bone or joint information. Stay far away from these tests.

q.name: Don’t know what to say here for this one...

content.p: Don’t know what to say here for this one...

q.name: So I want to start by saying this process was LONG and the customer service is useless so in turn it…

content.p: So I want to start by saying this process was LONG and the customer service is useless so in turn it
I sent my test in 3/2/2020 they received it 3/20/2020 the status changed to “resulted” on 4/1/2020 but it must have been a prank because they were not posted until 4/4/2020.

On 4/2 I contacted them trying to figure out if there was some kind of error going on and that’s why I couldn’t see the results. Customer service basically said it’s a combination test kit and each part apparently takes 3-4 weeks so I could still have another month for results.

I was confused by this as it said “resulted” in both rows. When I asked them to clarify the process and why it would say resulted if there weren’t indeed results. Instead of answering they gave me the email to the vet and told me to contact him.

I never received any emails from Orivet as they promised except for the welcome email when I registered the barcodes (and yes I checked my spam-nothing there either). Customer service DEFINITELY gets 1 star all the way. They either don’t answer questions and tell you it’s out of their hands or give you the scripted “it takes 3-4 weeks”

Ease of use is a 4. It’s easy to set up and I didn’t have any issue with the 2 different bar codes like others did. The picture on the Inside of the box as well as the website help you (or at least me) navigate that part rather easy. Getting my dog to allow me to do FOUR swabs on her? Not so easy -tip: do two at a time under the side lip.

The results themselves 5. They’re very comprehensive and informative both on breed as well as health risks/concerns. Definitely put us at easy about a lot of things for our pup but also let us know about things we should focus on a bit more.
Overall a 4 star review does seem too generous to me, however a 3 star review seems too harsh given the fact that this is indeed a review of the product and not the company itself.
If I could give it a 3.5 I would, but alas I cannot so 4 stars it is.
Just ordered one for our cat, fingers crossed that it’ll be less frustrating.

q.name: Recommended item

content.p: Recommended item

q.name: The sample is collected and ready to go out in the mail and I am VERY excited to see what all kinds…

content.p: The sample is collected and ready to go out in the mail and I am VERY excited to see what all kinds of DNA went into the creation of our Wolly Mammoth looking dog. We have
had him from birth and owned his Mother & Grandmother but have no idea about Dad or Grandpa, both were mystery dogs that snuck in during the night somehow, seriously.

We want to know what kind of dog grows hair THAT long and fuzzy, he has hair nearly 8 inches long and he is a big dog so he has been kind of a mystery to us from day one, all the other pups looked like German shepherds and he was the oddball. This is a fun process! I can't wait to get the results!
If this review helped you make an informed buying choice please let me know by leaving a Helpful vote. I hope you have a WONDERFUL shopping experience!

q.name: Super easy to use - the results seem to be pretty accurate

content.p: Super easy to use - the results seem to be pretty accurate

q.name: I was hoping to upload photos of the process, but I'm not seeing an options for that. The swabs look…

content.p: I was hoping to upload photos of the process, but I'm not seeing an options for that. The swabs look like little mascara brushes so they are easy to slip into the dog's mouth and just sort of scrape the cheek. I swabbed, put in the self addressed stamped envelope, and dropped off at the post office. 30 days later I logged in, and got results that seemed pretty accurate. I am happy I found out he has a sensitivity to one of his heart worm medications. I was able to show it to the vet and we ended up changing his meds out of an abundance of caution. He didn't show a propensity for any underlying medical conditions, but he is a little chunky :-)

q.name: A Little Confusing but Accurate

q.name: I was so thrilled to get this test- and the results are spot on. My dog is a mix of Coton and Havane…

content.p: I was so thrilled to get this test- and the results are spot on. My dog is a mix of Coton and Havanese and the results came back that he is exactly 50/50. I also love all of the
genetic disease testing. I think that will prove helpful later.

Having said that, the instructions, barcode, and swabbing was all so confusing. So much so, that I did it on the weekend and had to call the next week to get clarification. It came with two sets of codes and I was unclear on which one to use and where. After calling, they said that one was from an old system of barcoding. I ended up using both. My pooch wasn't crazy about using the swabs but it was brief and painless, so he got over it quick. The turnover time isn't bad, considering that we have had the corona virus going on...

q.name: I like Wisdon Panel better

content.p: I like Wisdon Panel better

q.name: I have had my three other dog's DNA tested using Widom Panel. I am comparing this Orivet dog DNA tes…

content.p: I have had my three other dog's DNA tested using Widom Panel. I am comparing this Orivet dog DNA testing kit to my experience with the Wisdom Panel.

When I got the Orivet kit in the mail the box was already half-open it was not sealed in any way. I wasn't even sure that it wasn't a retuned item as after opening the ID bar code sticker was not where it was supposed to be and there were no paper instructions just vague instructions printed on the inside of the box. Since I've done this before I knew what to do but placing which barcode where and using the barcode to activate the kit initially before sending it in was not clear. After swabbing my dog's cheek, there is no place on the box to let the swabs dry. Wisdom Panel has a place in its box to allow the swabs to dry which is very convenient. There are 2 sets of barcodes in the box so after trial and error, I was able to activate this kit. After a few weeks, I was sent an email telling me the wellness plan of my dog but there was no explanation included and as far as I can see there is anything on their website.
I had emailed them to see if there was any further information on their website but I did not get any response.
I also had to email them again because they said my dog's breed results were ready but after logging in the results said pending. After a few days, someone emailed me the results.

As far as the test results I am a little surprised as I got my dog when she was 8 weeks. I met her mom and siblings and was told by the owner that she was Pekingese and the father is a Miniature Pinscher. The results from Orivet do not mention Miniature Pinscher at all and lists four other breeds and Pekingese as 12.5%. The disappointing part was the 25% unknown mixed breed.
I definitely plan to have her DNA tested using another testing kit either Wisdom Panel or Embark. I would not use Orivet again.

q.name: Takes forever and doesn't cover all variations

content.p: Takes forever and doesn't cover all variations

q.name: While we didn't experience the same issues others have, I still was not fully satisfied by this gene…

content.p: While we didn't experience the same issues others have, I still was not fully satisfied by this genetic service. It really suffers from not being able to give similar results as the human DNA services. It took several weeks to get results back and when we finally did, the results were not satisfying. Not because we had other expectations, but because one half of our dog's family tree was missing. She is most certainly a mixed breed, like humans. But they gave us three different breeds for one side of her lineage, but no results for the other side. Seriously. 50% was left unanswered. The other half was three breeds we found very interesting, so it wasn't a total loss. But overall, I can't say this service is worth over $100. Perhaps the science needs to advance a bit further.
