q.name: Dog DNA Test

content.p: Dog DNA Test

q.name: We adopted our dog from a rescue five years ago. She was one of a littler of five that had been aban…

content.p: We adopted our dog from a rescue five years ago. She was one of a littler of five that had been abandoned by the side of the road in Arkansas. Along with her siblings, she was pulled from a high kill shelter from a rescue group and transported to a foster home in Connecticut. One week later, we were lucky enough to adopt her and have since been enjoying life with the best dog in the world.

I've always been curious about my dog's parentage. She looks like some kind of hound and shepherd mix. She's 60 pounds with a short coat and the kind of black and tan coloring typical to German Shepherds. The rescue had her listed as a hound mix, which was their best guess based on her looks.

When I had the chance to try this test, I was very excited to think of what we might learn. The test was easy to use and the directions were clear. There were cotton swabs that I wiped and swirled around in my dog's cheeks. She was cooperative but clearly thought I was crazy during this process. The swabs are left to air dry for several minutes before being closed up in plastic pouches. Bar codes for each test (breed test and health test) are included. You do need to activate the test on the Orivet website before mailing.

Once you mail in your test, you can check the progress of your dog's DNA on the website. The results are presented in a clear chart. My dog turned out to be a true mutt, so much so that one of her parents couldn't even be identified. Her other parent was apparently a mix of golden retriever, labrador retriever, doberman pinscher, and unknown mixed breed. This means she's still over 60% mystery dog, which isn't very surprising. Dog breeds are all basically mixes of other dog breeds, so with any DNA test, you need to be prepared for some unknowns.

The other aspect of this health is the genetic test screen. The test looks for 180 genetic diseases or disorders, and happily my super mutt tested negative for all of them. She is also a carrier for 0 genetic diseases and at risk for 0 genetic diseases. It was also pretty fascinating to look through the genetic traits to see which ones she carries. Not surprisingly, her traits show her as a short haired black and tan dog.

So, while the mystery of my dog isn't completely solved, it was fun to learn at least some of her genetic history. I also found the genetic disease profile very comforting -- my dog's health should be good as long as we continue to take good care of her.

A friend of mine bought a puppy from a pet store that was supposed to be a purebred golden retriever. The puppy most likely came from a puppy mill and has some behavior issues. My friend is also suspicious that the dog is not a purebred golden. Now that the dog is full grown, she has some appearance traits that don't seem consistent to the breed. She is considering a DNA test just to be sure -- they love the dog and would keep her regardless, but would also like some answers. A test like this could be a valuable resource for them.

I have not yet shared these test results with our vet, but at my dog's next checkup I'll ask if they'd like to see the genetic profile. When filling out your dog's profile, you can enter in the dates of vaccines and flea/tick/heartworm preventive. Your pet's Orivet profile will let you know when these preventive measures need to be updated.

Overall, this is a valuable resource for dog owners who want to make sure their pet is healthy for years to come. It helps you keep track of vaccines and other preventatives and can give you some insight into your dog's breed traits. My dog might only be 25% retriever, but she sure does have a very strong retrieving instinct. She'll play fetch for as long as any of us have the energy to throw tennis balls or sticks. She's very smart, protective, loyal, and affectionate. I do wish we knew exactly what her mix is, because she's the perfect family dog and anyone who could recreate her traits would be credited for inventing the world's most ideal breed of dog.

q.name: Not easy to get sample and website needs a lot of work

content.p: Not easy to get sample and website needs a lot of work

q.name: The sample kit was confusing when I got it. There were lots of barcode stickers and packs of swabs.…

content.p: The sample kit was confusing when I got it. There were lots of barcode stickers and packs of swabs. I used the same barcode sticker for all, but later seems it should be one pack is for one barcode and other is for the other barcode. But box didn't mention anything about two. I emailed them and they seemed to not worry and in the end I got all the results. Getting the samples was not easy. They were like metal brush (like pipe cleaners) and I could not tell if they were wet or not and my dog (13 pound small dog) did not like having them put in her mouth so this was not easy trying to hold her and get sample. I used a treat to get her to stay and drool more (did not give her treats until after I got sample; since it said not to feed prior).

I mailed the samples in the box (from NYC to Connecticut). I mailed it on Feb 26, Got notified they received it on March 6, got notified that the health test was done on March 17 and finally parents and stuff was ready on March 21. Not bad in my opinion and this was all during the virus drama in the tri-state.

Their website needs lots of work. Like updating account profile says it is saved but nothing ever changes. Like I uploaded the photo again since every place showed It sideways (it was not sideways on my local machine). But it never changes. Their grid on the page listing the animals has a dropdown but it has overflow hidden so you can't see the options unless you go into dev tools and fix it... Just confusing and complicated stuff like that.

Once I got into the results things were better. You can see the results for my dog in the pictures. A lot of it was "unknown" but it breaks down what they think it was mostly which was nice. And I am happy that my dogs genetic health says she has no carrier issues or anything. The health plan was useful to see; but the information was pretty generic (maybe if your dog has some of the health issues from genetic that changes accordingly). But the stuff was pretty normal that you would do for your dog anyway.

I really like the parent map and finding out about my dog (she was a rescue we got 2 years ago) and it was nice to see her genetics and breed makeup. I found it interesting and you can share the results with family and friends which is nice.

Overall getting results was hard; but even though I feared I didn't get a good results it went through so that is good. And the results were fun and informative. I think this has a lot of promise if they make their kit more straightforward and less confusing and then fix their website to make sure everything works.

q.name: Some insightful information but not like I thought

content.p: Some insightful information but not like I thought

q.name: This is an awesome overall idea for giving us some insight into our furry friends that we love...it…

content.p: This is an awesome overall idea for giving us some insight into our furry friends that we love...it just does not tell me as much as I had hoped. The results are very specific, do not really compare a lot to genetic testing for humans like I've encountered, but do have some good information regarding serious health issues (but the potential issues seem to be rather obscure and not what I have ever heard of). The kit took a good while to turn around, which I expected because this kind of thing takes time, but it did not really give me a lot of information about the breed and things like that. What I found was interesting is that you are basically supposed to indicate the breed on the paperwork you are sending there. Nonetheless, the process was not fun to get the sample, this is not cheap at all, and the results are less than what I hoped. This is a great first step towards getting pet owners some information closer to what we can get for ourselves but it is just a bit too costly for me to do again with the results.
Getting the sample:

This was not easy as you have to make sure the cat has not eaten within a while before getting the sample. The sample is on a stick kind of thing and you have to rub it in the cat's mouth for 15 seconds for each little stick. That is not a fun experience, nor is it easy to do. Thankfully, my cat played nicely and allowed me to get the samples for them to be sent off. You have to make sure there isn't any visible food on the stick (not as easy as it sounds and...gag) and you have to let the sticks stand up and not touch anything for a while until they dry before you pack them. This is another hurdle that I can imagine people struggling a bit with...or at least I did.
Results:

They took about a month to come in. Oddly enough, it took a few weeks for them to say they got it but I got the results like a week after that. The info is interesting but I would not say I would do it again.

Overall, if you worry your cat has some kind of congenital issue that could be identified via this test, it could be an option and I would love to recommend this but I think it has a little ways to go. I love that this is an option but I wish it had some more info.

content.p: I adopted a rescue pup that my family and I were constantly trying to guess how big she was going to get and what kind of dogs she was blended from. I chose the Wisdom DNA due to the added bonus of any hereditary issues she may have. Capturing the DNA was fairly easy as it was simply swabbing the inside of her cheeks. I received the results within 2 weeks time which was quicker than I expected. It was just so much fun to see all of the breeds that were in her DNA. The kit isn’t cheap but honestly worth every Penny of just knowing rather than constantly trying to guess. The accuracy of all of her expected traits were spot on! I would highly recommend this to folks who want to spend a little extra on knowing where their beloved furry friend came from.

q.name: Embark results are very comprehensive and i love a lot of the features offered such as being able to…

content.p: Embark results are very comprehensive and i love a lot of the features offered such as being able to see DNA relatives.

However I was very confused with by the breed result. DNA and gene inheritance is very complex, but there were several things that did not add up. My dog returned a 50% primary result of German Shepherd, and secondary results of 22% Australian cattle dog, 6% cocker spaniel, and 21% Supermutt (Best guesses they provided for super mutt were belgian malanois, Chinook, Dutch shepherd, Berger Picard.)

In general appearance, my dog does not match at all with either the primary or secondary breeds. Her ears flop down, she is wire haired, and has bearded muzzle, eyebrow face furnishings. She looks absolutely nothing akin to the other mixed match German shepherd/australian cattle dog breed mixes. She does bare a close resemblance to the berger picard guess in her supermutt mix, however that was--at most-- only 20% of her DNA.

I have not used a different testing company to compare results, but i am skeptical of the breeds provided by embark. I often get asked about what breed she is (due to get unique characteristics) and always receive confused looks when i answer that she is a German shepherd cattle dog mix.
If I had to guess, i would think she is a mix of German shepherd and poodle or has some mixture of a German Wirehaired Pointer.

q.name: Treat Yo’self

content.p: Treat Yo’self

q.name: Pricier than the other DNA kits, but worth it. I would recommend purchasing the health kit initially…

content.p: Pricier than the other DNA kits, but worth it. I would recommend purchasing the health kit initially, if that something you’re interested in. (It’s twice the price to add it on later, but if you email and ask you can get a 50% off code. Ends up being the same price as buying it in the first place.)

In any case, it was very easy to swab. The kit directions were super clear. They do email you when they receive your kit, and when the analysis has started. It does not feel as though your swab has gone off into the ozone.

The results are easy to understand, and they come with a personalized video of your pup’s family tree. It did take a little longer than the other test, but I also purchased it around Christmas so I’m sure there was a rush. I am hopeful to find siblings, I think the “Relatives” is a really fun feature.

I would have sworn my pupper was part husky or malamute; turns out she is mainly cattle dog and chowchow. I have another cattle dog mix, so I’m familiar with some of the quirks. Having this knowledge will certainly help guide her training. (Is it just me, or does it seem like everyone is cattle dog nowadays?)

I have read other reviews, where there were complaints about customer service. I can tell you that was not my experience. It takes a few hours for someone to get back to you, but you will receive a return email with your inquiry specifically answered. They don’t send you some random canned response. For example, I emailed and asked about the status of my swab. A customer service representative actually looked it up, and advised me that the analysis was completed by the lab, and they were just assembling the report. The particular ETA she gave for my results were accurate.

It was certainly a worthwhile purchase. So many people are adopting rescues during the pandemic and it’s wonderful. This kit allows you to indulge your inner “crazy dog parent” in a fun and informative way.
Treat yo’self- buy it and swab your fur baby, and then wait obsessively for the results... There’s nothing else to do anyway.

q.name: I wanted to see what possible medical issues I might face in the future due to genetics. I was caref…

content.p: I wanted to see what possible medical issues I might face in the future due to genetics. I was careful to not tell anything about my dog, as a way of determining the accuracy of the test. It came back saying she was 100% Golden Retriever, which she is. It also said she had wavy hair and should weigh in the neighborhood of 73 pounds. Both of these are also true. These things convinced me that the test is accurate. I also learned that she has two genetic issues that are indicated. One is a non-issue because it is recessive and she only cares one gene, the other is a warning of possible erroneous lab results that could happen if the vet is not aware of it. Thanks to the test, my vet now knows of the issue and can watch out for it.
I would recommend this as an easy, accurate genetic test.

Embark gave the whole picture, though the numbers were very different on most of the breeds identified. That makes wonder who is more correct. The fur color and size were off. Otherwise, it seems pretty good with eye color, coat length, etc. There is also no Akbash breed in their list, though that is more common in the US now. Overall I am satisfied.

content.p: The DNA analysis was extremely detailed. The health information was very helpful and we will provide it to our vet since it confirms our dog carries genes for a canine DM which we already suspected he had.

However, the best part about the test and most fun was the relatives. We adopted our dog from a rescue and all we knew about him was that he used to live in Greenville, SC. Well the results found an immediate family member in Greenville, SC. We had not provided any information whatsoever about our dog to Embark before receiving the results of the test. This confirmed the accuracy of the test for us without a doubt. We would definitely do the Embark test again for any future rescues we adopt. We have a friend who used a different company for their DNA test and their results were not nearly as detailed.

q.name: I got my cat a little over three years ago. I had to have my boxer put to sleep and was devastated.…

content.p: I got my cat a little over three years ago. I had to have my boxer put to sleep and was devastated. My sister called me the next day and said she had a cat for me. My sister is a teacher and one of her students was living in a homeless shelter and wasn’t able to keep the kitten she had found. I hadn’t a had a cat in several years and immediately told her I didn’t want one. She swore that this cat “was different” and I needed to see him. Anyway, I ended up falling in love with him. I suspected that he was part maine coon but wasn’t entirely sure. The breed report was incredibly helpful and lined up with what I suspected. It does take a while but overall I am happy I did it. It took nine weeks from when I originally mailed the sample and just over six weeks from when they received it.
12/29/20 – Dropped the kit off at the post office.
1/15/21 – Received an email that my CatKit was received (not sure why it took so long since it was only travelling 30 miles). 1/28/21 – Received an email that the DNA sample was being sequenced.
2/9/21 – Received an email that sequencing was complete and there is only one more step (ETA 5-10 days).
2/22/21 – Received an email that my report is running late.
3/2/21 – Report available.

The results are quite interesting: he is around 10% rag doll (shocking) and 10% American shorthair (make more sense to me) and 7% Maine coon. my cat is very large, I am happy to know he has some large cats' gene such as ragdoll or Maine coon, but he does not look like a longhair cat at all. After reading one of the reviews that stated that a customer had contacted Basepaws concerning their tests that did not show accurate results, and because of Basepaws small database, I am wondering if that is why we have no results showing her being part Bengal? Actually part of the DNA test kit that was easy to use was the Q-tip applicator to obtain the sample from our kitty.

q.name: As any DNA test, the results may vary, but overall we are happy that we did it.

content.p: As any DNA test, the results may vary, but overall we are happy that we did it.

q.name: I think our expectations outweighed the scientific possibilities available right now for DNA testing…

content.p: I think our expectations outweighed the scientific possibilities available right now for DNA testing so for what is possible, we are happy with the results. We received exactly what was marketed to us; a possible* DNA profile of our cat with the addition of some testing for genetic markers for diseases/disorders. I was curious as to whether they would find allergies in our cat since he has them, but we also bought the lower cost one that may not have had that included.
Ultimately we knew there was some sort of large, wild cat in our rescue cat, and we are happy to learn that he has at least part Savannah and Maine Coon within him.
We expected a little more of a wild cat variety considering his size, colors, and behaviors but maybe we are just overenthusiastic cat owners!

content.p: I've always been kind of skeptical about these kinds of kits, but I am glad I bought one for my rescue pup. The breed results seemed accurate based on her appearance, and I was surprised to learn the shelter's guess about her breed was fairly inaccurate. Keep in mind that if your dog is a mixed-breed, there may be a category called "Super Mutt." This is basically a big combination of different breeds that are so diluted they can't be analyzed. The genetic health screen is thorough and put my mind at ease. There are reports that can be printed for your vet.
Overall, the process was very easy. The company keeps you informed at every step, and the results online have thorough explanations. The results s took about 3 weeks from the day I sent in the swab.

q.name: Best money spent!

content.p: Best money spent!

q.name: We rescued our girl after she was dumped at around 5 weeks old. We knew nothing about her other than…

content.p: We rescued our girl after she was dumped at around 5 weeks old. We knew nothing about her other than she was very sick. Embark allowed us to learn so much about our puppy and know how to better her life not only by finding out what breed she is but by finding out her health risks. Without Embark our girl would forever be a mystery.

content.p: The results matched in some cases what we thought and surprised us with others that we could see in Bella when we looked. I also appreciated the heads-up about possible health issues and had them checked out. I wish this had been available when we got our older dog. We wouldn't have been surprised by his seizures and other health issues, some of which we could have started treating before they got bad. Thank you.

We were excited to find out what breed our dog was, but never in our wildest dreams did we think we'd connect with her litter of puppies found abandoned in a canyon 2 years ago. The joy we've experienced with the family-tree side of Embark (which other brands don't offer) has simply been immeasurable!
Our results took about a month, but then again, we did it right after Christmas which I'm guessing is one of their busiest times of year. It was well worth the wait!

q.name: I adopted a bonded pair of male lab mixes found wandering the streets in Mississippi. They are both…

content.p: I adopted a bonded pair of male lab mixes found wandering the streets in Mississippi. They are both obviously labs with something else. I don't care what their lineage is really but was curious. Easy to swab, Embark kept me informed of what was going on the whole time. I was surprised because I would have sworn one of the boys was a black lab and Rhodesian Ridgeback mix but was wrong. I asked if they could tell me if they were related and they said "sure, request it after the results are back." Turns out they are probably cousins. Make since since their DNA results are similar. Embark is a wonderful organization as far as I am concerned.

content.p: This is such a neat product. It is easy to use. I recommend completing the test when your pooch is heading to bed for the night because you need to take two samples from the mouth, holding the wand in the dog's cheek and gum line for 15 seconds. The packaging is simple, with paid postage and you just pop the results in the mail. We received ours within two weeks. You register online and the results come by email. Our mix had 12 breeds broken down by percentages and information on each breed presented in an accessible, nice looking way. So happy we used this test because it has given us insight into all kinds of aspects of our puppy from behavior, to potential size, health etc.

q.name: I was really excited about the test when I got it. I had recently adopted a dog that people say look…

content.p: I was really excited about the test when I got it. I had recently adopted a dog that people say looks like Tramp from Lady and the Tramp. It was easy to use though he wasn’t the biggest fan of me brushing his gums. When I got my test results back I ended up calling Wisdom Panel to tell them I believe the results were completely off. I don’t see my dogs biggest result to be a chihuahua. Wisdom Panel then had me send them photos of my dog. All I got back in response was we checked and we still think the same thing. I would use a different dog dna test. Also don’t waste your time on the one that gives you medical results, it’s bogus. Luckily Amazon was great and refunded my money.

q.name: Easy to use. Results seem very accurate to me. I tested a shelter puppy I adopted. They guessed she…

content.p: Easy to use. Results seem very accurate to me. I tested a shelter puppy I adopted. They guessed she was German Shepherd and Labrador retriever mix.
Results (here’s the top 4 but she had 9 breeds total): 26% Rottweiler
26% Doberman pincher
26% German Shepherd
13% Labrador retriever

She looks just like that mix and it makes total sense. While I like all those dogs, I doubt I would have gotten a full blood Rottweiler or Doberman. But the mix is a super sweet, affectionate, happy, cuddly, dog and people living girl. :). I have noticed more alert barking as she’s gotten older (she’s almost 6 months old as I write this), but she’s not an incessant annoying barker. She’s just perfect!

Anyway, I sent off the results for Embark. Those of you researching the two, I haven’t had my results from embark to talk about that, but I can tell you they make it super easy to share the results. You can download multiple reports and even click share. No way to export your report from Wisdompanel. If accuracy is assumed to be similar between the two, I would pay the little bit extra for embark. I want my own personal copy in her files offline rather than stored on their website. Plus it’s nice to share with friends and family.

q.name: Real-life Lady & the Tramp Story written in her DNA!

content.p: Real-life Lady & the Tramp Story written in her DNA!

q.name: They were so helpful with the collection process. The results were faster than expected, fascinating…

content.p: They were so helpful with the collection process. The results were faster than expected, fascinating, and extremely beneficial. I adopted Maya six months ago. She is 10 years old. She had breast cancer when she was taken to the shelter after her previous owner died. They did a mastectomy. She is now cancer-free. Maya is the best dog I've ever had in every way. I was both curious about her breeds and concerned about her health. It was very helpful and reassuring to see that Maya is more genetically diverse than most dogs and has none of the variants for the more than 211 genetic health preconditions tested. The shelter is interested in seeing her results, and I will also share them with her veterinarian. I feel like I can ensure her longest, healthiest life with this info. And I never would've guessed that her parents are a real-life Lady & the Tramp story! She must look just like Dear-old Dad. Maya's mother was a pure-bred Cocker Spaniel (51% of her DNA), and her father was 25% Miniature Pinscher, 21% Parson Russell Terrier, and 3% Stumpy Tail Australian Cattledog.

q.name: I've had Vinnie for over 5 years and always wondered what breed he was. This brand of DNA test was h…

content.p: I've had Vinnie for over 5 years and always wondered what breed he was. This brand of DNA test was highly recommended by multiple vets. It is the most accurate dog breed test kit on the market!

q.name: Quick, Easy and Accurate

content.p: Quick, Easy and Accurate

q.name: The process of getting the saliva sample and mailing it in was very, very easy. I was sent notificat…

content.p: The process of getting the saliva sample and mailing it in was very, very easy. I was sent notifications along the way regarding the various steps in analyzing her DNA, so I always know that progress was being made. The results came back quicker than I had thought they would, and they seemed to be very accurate. I always knew Maggie was a pit bull mix, but I couldn't imagine what else. When I got her from the rescue program, they thought she was a Lab/Pitt mix, however, she hates the water and has never retrieved anything in her life!! When I saw the picture and description of the Australian Cattle dog, I immediately knew that was correct!

q.name: Seems accurate, quick results

content.p: Seems accurate, quick results

q.name: We chose to do the DNA testing on our pup because of his merle coloring. We purchased from a breeder…

content.p: We chose to do the DNA testing on our pup because of his merle coloring. We purchased from a breeder who said our boy is an F1b goldendoodle (mom is 100% poodle - solid black, dad is 1/2 golden 1/2 poodle - merle coloring). His genetic results showed 92.6 % poodle and 7.4 % golden. There was no Australian Shepherd (which is what we were questioning) or any other breed that carries merle historically. The percentages didn’t seem to add up the way I expected, but nevertheless we have a goldendoodle. I’d definitely recommend Embark. We had fast results and are very pleased.

content.p: Package came quickly, instructions were easy to follow, and we received our results back within 4 weeks. They were accurate results for what we assumed our dog was, and the email updates were great!!! Great customer service on the side of Embark.

comment.p: SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer takes an active response to their pet’s test results.

code.name: BEHAVIORAL RESPONSE: Altered pet care and/or husbandry

comment.p: Consumer takes an active response to their pet’s test results and changes their pet’s medical care, husbandry, and/or behavioral management.
Consumer actively responds to test results in another way not specified in another code.

code.name: BEHAVIORAL RESPONSE: Shared Results with Someone

comment.p: Consumer indicates genetic test results led them to consult with a veterinarian, behaviorist, and/or genetic counsellor.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer provides context to their experience using the product.
Consumers references their use of the product, an aspect or the entire process, as being effortless and relatively simple.
Consumer conveys they enjoyed their use of the genetic test.

code.name: CONSUMER EXPERIENCE: Not easy to use

comment.p: Consumer conveys they had difficulty in some capacity with using the product. This can be related to sample collection, receiving results, or accessing their accounts.
Consumer provides context to their experience using the product other than it being easy to use or entertaining.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer shares sentiment about the monetary value of the genetic test.

Consumer indicated the test should have a lower monetary cost, product's monetary cost does not match the value of services rendered, and/or they did not agree with the genetic test price matching the benefits and/or value.
Consumer indicated the product's monetary cost matched or exceeded the value of services rendered.

SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer shares their experience with using the genetic test company’s customer service. This includes interaction with (or lack of) company associates in a customer service capacity.
Consumer shares an unfavorable experience with using the genetic test company’s customer experience. This includes customer service not being communicative and/or not being helpful.
Consumer shares a favorable experience with using the company’s customer service.
Pet owner indicated they received results they anticipated.
Pet owner indicated they received results they did not foresee.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer discusses their ability to translate their pet’s genetic test results or deciphering the format in which it was presented to them.
Consumer indicates they have used the genetics test to interpret behavioral outcomes of their pet.
Consumer indicates they have used the genetics test to interpret breed related aspects of their pet.
Consumer indicates they have used the genetics test to interpret health related outcomes of their pet.
Consumer translates their pet’s genetic test results in terms of other related outcome that do not include health, behavior, or breed.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer’s reasons for purchasing a genetic test.
Reviewer indicates they were motivated to pursue pet genetic testing to learn about their pet.
Reviewer indicates they were motivated to pursue pet genetic testing for amusement or pleasure.
Reviewer indicates they were motivated to pursue pet genetic testing for other reasons not related to education or entertainment.
Consumer made a general statement about the company, product, and/or process needing to be revised.
Pet owner indicates having completed the pet genetic testing gave them solace or reassurance.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer shares their personal understanding about the genetic test company and/or the genetic test itself.

Consumer shared an attitude of perceiving their pet's results as being correct or without error.
3/16/21, 1:42 PM, merged with PERCEPTION: Trust Consumer identified they believed their pet's results and/or indicated they viewed the results as dependable.
Consumer indicated their pet's genetic test results were difficult to interpret.

Consumer shared an attitude of perceiving their pet's genetic test results as flawed or incorrect.
3/16/21, 1:42 PM, merged with PERCEPTION: Do not trust Pet owner identified they did not believe their pet's results and/or the results were not dependable.
Consumer indicated the test results shared usable information.
Consumer indicated the genetic test report did not share any usable information and/or the consumer perceived the results to not have value.
Reviewer shares another belief or attitude about the genetic company and/or genetic test not related to accuracy, informative, or confusing.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer shares their experience with receiving their pet's results.
Consumer shares receiving the results had a quick turn around time.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer shares their experience with receiving their pet's results.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer shares their experience with receiving their pet's results.
Consumer indicates they have reviewed the product without having received their pet's results at the time of the review.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer is eithering promoting or not promoting the product to other consumers in their review.
Consumer recommends a competitor's product over the product they are reviewing.
Consumer explicitly states they recommend others use the genetic test they are reviewing.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer shares a sentiment about using the product.
Reviewer shares a sentiment that could be interpreted as a negative or positive experience with using the product.
Review is misleading, nonsensical, or is clearly not related to the genetic test being reviewed.
Review highlights the pros and cons of the product for others to make an informed decision about using the product.

Sentiment expressing a disapproval and/or bad outcome with using the product. This includes consumers indicating they used or would use a competitor's product and/or not recommending the product for others to use.
Sentiment to express approval and/or a good experience with using the product. This also includes consumers stating they would use the product again and/or recommending for others to use.
Amazon labelled review as being authored by a consumer who received the genetic test for free or the reviewer received a reward for writing a review.
SEMANTIC DOMAIN: Consumer communicates information to other potential consumers related to their pet’s demographics and/or their pet’s genetic test results.
Consumer provided details about their pet such as acquisition, characteristics, etc.
Consumer shares specific details obtained from their pet's genetic testing results.
Consumer shared they used a competitor's product to compare the reviewed company's results to make a determination about the accuracy or trust worthiness of the company being reviewed.
